briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 10, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Unprecedented Climate Crisis, A Global Warning
sections.content: Today's news clearly revealed the severity of the climate crisis spreading globally. The United States recorded its hottest March in 132 years, experiencing a combination of abnormal heat and drought [12]. The Philippines is also enduring a record-breaking heatwave, with perceived temperatures reaching a 'dangerous' level of 42°C [115]. The Copernicus Climate Change Service announced that globally, March was the fourth warmest on record, warning of accelerating global warming [109, 114]. Ocean warming is pushing emperor penguins and Antarctic fur seals towards extinction [98, 102, 103, 110], and concerns have also been raised that it could trigger methane feedback loops, further accelerating climate change [116]. Vanuatu is proposing a UN climate change resolution, urging the international community's attention to climate responsibility [99].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Adaptation and Disaster Preparedness Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, proactive efforts to respond to the impacts of climate change are active. The Presidential Committee on Climate Crisis Response is serving as the control tower for climate crisis response [1], and the Administrator of the Rural Development Administration visited agricultural sites in the Gyeongnam region to address climate change [2]. In particular, Goyang City is proposing and recommending adjustments to rice transplanting periods to adapt to climate change, aiming to enhance the agricultural sector's climate resilience [3, 5]. Furthermore, Incheon Seo-gu has strengthened its summer disaster countermeasures in response to climate change [4], and the Korea Green Foundation held a parliamentary debate to ensure the health rights of asthmatic children in the era of climate crisis [13], accelerating adaptation efforts across society. Meanwhile, an emergency has been declared for a direct hit of 'sweltering heat and typhoons' this summer due to unprecedented sea temperature rises around the Korean Peninsula, necessitating even more thorough preparations [16].

sections.title: 🌱 Transition to a Carbon-Neutral Society: Participation of Local Communities and Businesses
sections.content: Efforts across various regions in South Korea continue towards achieving carbon neutrality goals. Pohang City unveiled its blueprint for hosting COP and launched its Climate Crisis Response Committee for the first time, setting the great transformation of carbon-neutral industries as a key objective [6, 11, 17, 18, 43, 49, 51]. Gwangju City's launch ceremony for 'Bitgoeul Net-Zero Friends Season 2' [19, 36, 37] and the voluntary participation of 40 Cheongju Green Villages in greenhouse gas reduction [14, 40, 46] are excellent examples of citizen-led carbon-neutral practices spreading. Korea Southern Power is contributing to climate crisis response by creating a 'Carbon Neutral Forest' to commemorate its 25th anniversary [21, 26, 27, 53], and Gunpo City is leading the way in carbon-neutral practices by recycling street tree wood waste [29, 44, 52, 96], accelerating the carbon-neutral transition in various sectors. New approaches are also being explored, such as initiating a circular economy and achieving carbon neutrality through waste shoe recycling [39], and attending a forum on clean methanol as a carbon-neutral fuel [41, 48].

sections.title: ⚠️ Renewable Energy Transition: Progress and Challenges
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is emerging as a key challenge for energy security and climate crisis response. The South Chungcheong Provincial Council passed an ordinance to foster the renewable energy industry, laying the groundwork for the spread of resident-participatory renewable energy [56, 75, 76, 82, 90, 91, 93, 95]. Yeosu City is recruiting applicants for its new and renewable energy support projects [62, 66, 86, 88], and specific efforts to expand renewable energy supply are underway, such as the implementation of a public EV charging fee discount policy during daytime on weekends [65, 73]. However, the Green Transition Institute pointed out that 'expanding renewable energy alone has limitations' and emphasized the need to break dependence on fossil fuels in the building and transportation sectors [59, 68]. Furthermore, energy shocks originating from the Middle East are demanding an acceleration of energy strategy restructuring, encompassing nuclear power, coal, and renewable energy [80, 81, 83]. As a forum on electricity regulation governance is scheduled to prepare for the '100 GW Renewable Energy Era' [57, 60, 69, 71, 78], in-depth discussions appear necessary to establish a stable renewable energy system.

briefing.closing: Considering the climate crisis situations discussed in today's briefing and the various domestic and international response efforts, the urgency and importance of climate action are being emphasized more than ever. This concludes today's climate briefing.
